title: Stenospermum corifolium
taxonomy: Stenospermum corifolium is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is native to Australia and is commonly known as the 'Abundant Life Plant' due to its unique ability to produce flowers and fruits simultaneously. This plant is also known as 'Abundant life plant' or 'Bitter orange'. Stenospermum corifolium is often grown as an ornamental plant and is prized for its attractive foliage and vibrant flowers. The plant's classification is also supported by its position in the APG III classification system.
uses: Stenospermum corifolium, also known as the dead man's fingers, has been reported to have potential medicinal uses, including treating various health conditions. intro: Stenospermum corifolium, commonly known as the 'Wormwood Bush' or 'Native Ash', is a shrub native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Stenospermum corifolium include sesquiterpenoid lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Stenospermum corifolium is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to treat various health conditions, or used as a topical application for wound healing.
